title: be going to (do)
desc: 계획된 의도 또는 근거 있는 예측
examples: Today, I'm going to show you my daily routine.
So I came to this stream and I'm going to warm up with some stretching first.
I'm going to walk for a minute to catch my breath.
I'm going to turn around and go back to our starting point.
I've always heard of runners high, but I just feel like I'm going to die.

title: will / shall
examples: I'll finish off with a few lunges.
I was going, if I see another runner, I'll usually nod my head or just raise my hand like that to say hello and kind of encourage each other.
Now I'm just cooling down and then I'll head home to make some breakfast.
Today I'm making breakfast tacos and I'll start by frying up some bacon.
I also had some nutritional yeast on hand, so I'll add that into.

title: When I do / When I've done
examples: In the US, when we go to a bar, we're there to drink.

title: can / could / be able to
examples: Whew, you can do it, Kelly.
I've got it on medium-high heat and I'm just going to drizzle a little bit of olive oil so that I can coat the bottom of it.
We ordered a lot of food because we couldn't make up our minds.
I can't choose a favor.
I can tell this place is going to be great.

title: may / might (possibility)
examples: This might be a stupid question.

title: have to / must
examples: But first things first, I have to take a drink of my coffee.
If I must choose, maybe I'll say the chimney cake actually.
You can, but you don't have to.

title: should (advice)
examples: Yeah, you should stir it because it's combination of jam, syrup, coffee, and heavy cream.

title: had better / it's time
examples: It's time to add in the liquid egg whites.
It's time to assemble the tacos.
